name: Pyramax Bank, Fsb
description_short: PyraMax Bank is a publicly traded community bank headquartered in Southeast Wisconsin serving personal and business customers since 1895, offering checking, savings, loans, and investment services.
description_long:
PyraMax Bank was originally founded on August 10, 1895, as South Milwaukee Savings Bank and merged with Mitchell Savings Bank in May 2000 to form its current identity. The bank's name combines "Pyra" from the pyramid-shaped logo of South Milwaukee Savings Bank and "Max" to signify maximized service. With more than $486 million in assets, PyraMax operates six convenient branch locations throughout southeastern Wisconsin and maintains a strong local presence while leveraging larger institutional financial power.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal banking products including checking and savings accounts, HSAs, IRAs, mortgages, and credit cards with rewards programs. They provide digital banking capabilities through online and mobile banking platforms, including bill payment via Bill Capture (photo submission through mobile app). Additional services include home equity loans, personal loans, and investment services through Cetera Investments. The bank emphasizes security features such as palm scanning identity verification at physical branches and transaction confirmation protocols.

PyraMax distinguishes itself as a depositor-owned community bank with local decision-making authority despite holding institutional-scale resources. The bank demonstrates community commitment through annual charitable donations exceeding $100,000 and employee volunteer programs. Their marketing emphasizes the combination of personal touch with modern technology, positioning themselves as "Your Financial Force" by merging community banking values with contemporary financial tools and digital capabilities.

As a publicly traded community bank, PyraMax serves a regional market primarily in Southeast Wisconsin and may have limited geographic availability outside this area. While they offer competitive products and demonstrate financial stability, consumers outside their service territory will need to verify branch access. The bank's focus on community banking means their product suite, while comprehensive, may lack some specialized offerings available at larger national institutions.
services: Personal checking accounts (Simply Cash Back Checking with rewards)
Personal savings accounts (Club Max Savings Accounts with prize rewards)
Home equity loans and lines of credit
Rewards credit cards (including Visa Zero+ Card with 0% APR promotion)
Mobile banking with Bill Capture feature
pros: Strong financial foundation with $486+ million in assets and over 125 years of operational history since 1895
Six convenient physical branch locations throughout Southeast Wisconsin with local decision-making authority
Modern digital banking features including mobile Bill Capture, online banking, digital wallets, and palm scan security verification
Competitive deposit products including Club Max Savings Accounts with prize rewards and Simply Cash Back Checking with $300 sign-up bonus
0% APR promotional credit card (Visa Zero+ Card) offering 21 months interest-free financing
Comprehensive personal banking services covering checking, savings, HSAs, IRAs, mortgages, and investment services
Community-focused bank donating $100,000+ annually to charities with active employee volunteer programs
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Southeast Wisconsin, making services inaccessible for customers outside the region
As a smaller regional bank, may lack specialized product offerings and white-glove services available at larger national institutions
Website content does not specify APR rates, fees, or competitive rate comparisons, requiring customers to call 414-421-8200 for specific pricing information
best_for: Southeast Wisconsin residents and business owners seeking personalized community banking with local decision-making
Customers prioritizing modern digital banking tools combined with in-person relationship banking at physical branches
Small to mid-sized businesses and individuals wanting to support a locally-owned, community-focused financial institution
diagnosis: PyraMax Bank is best for Southeast Wisconsin customers who value community banking relationships, local decision-making, and modern digital convenience in a stable, well-capitalized institution. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—the bank operates exclusively in southeastern Wisconsin, making it unsuitable for customers outside this region.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
